preview_text:
The model favors Ecuador heavily, assigning them an 82% chance of victory. Curaçao's win probability sits at just 1%, leaving the underdog facing a decisive disadvantage in the forecast.

The W/D/L distribution reflects a lopsided matchup. Ecuador's dominant modeling edge allows a meaningful draw probability at 16%, the scenario most likely to deny them three points. A Curaçao upset remains a tail-risk outcome in the model's view, making this a likely one-sided affair.

The model and market align substantially on Ecuador's superiority but diverge on the likelihood of alternative outcomes. The market raises Curaçao's win probability to 4% and compresses the draw to 10%, compared to the model's 1% and 16% respectively—a gap of roughly 3 percentage points on each. The sportsbook and prediction market consensus are nearly identical to each other, both more bullish on an Ecuador win than the model. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better reflects true match probability.
